titleStmt.title: SII 16.52: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 52. (A.R. No. 18 of 1915.) ŚRĪŚAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.

summary.p: On stones built into the floor of the platform in the eastern porch of the Mallikārjuna temple.
This is dated Śaka 1438 (current), Yuva, Śrāvaṇa śu. 15, Wednesday, lunar eclipse, corresponding regularly to 1515 A.D., July 25.

The record is important for the historical information contained in it. The king, it is stated, started out from Vijayanagara on a campaign of conquest towards the east, conquered at a stretch Udayagiri, Addaṅki, Vinukoṇḍa, Bellaṁkoṇḍa, Nāgārjunikoṇḍa, Taṅgēḍu, Kētavaram and other hill-forts and land-forts and captured Tirumala Kātarāya-mahāpātra. Having taken Koṇḍavīḍu, he captured alive Vīrabhadrarāya, Naraharidēva, Rāchūri Mallū-khānu, Uddaṇḍa-khānu, Jannāla-Kasavāpātra, Pūsapāṭi Rāchirāju, Śrīnātharāju, Lakshmīpatirāju, Paśchima-Bālachandra-Mahāpātra and others. Later he reinstated the captives in their places, visited Amarēśvara at Dharaṇikōta and performed the Tulāpurusha ceremony in the presence of the god on the banks of the river Kṛishṇavēṇi. He had the Rātnadhēnu and Sapta-sāgara mahādānas performed by his queens Chinnādēvi and Tirumaladēvi respectively. Finally he visited Śrīparvata, where he had the maṇḍapa built in the car-street of the temple. The epigraph refers to the gift of the villages Pāramañchāla and Ātukuru formerly made to the god of the place in the year Śrīmukha. It also mentions the remission of levies on loads (Kañchi-kāvaḍi), pack-horses, bullocks, asses and head-loads.

The details of the date given for the said Śrīmukha viz. Vaiśākha śu. 11 and Thursday do not agree for the year. But they work out correctly for the previous year Bhāva. Hence assuming the cyclic year to be a mistake for Bhāva, the date would correspond to 1514 A.D., May 4.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavarṣaṁbulu 1438
Agunēṁṭi yuva saṁvatsara śrāvaṇa śuddha 15 budhavārāna caṁdragrahaṇa
puṁṇyakālamaṁdu śrī mallikārjunamahādēvuniki śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rā
japaramēśvara mūrurāyaragaṁḍa Arirāyavibhāḍa Aṣṭadikkurāyamanōbhayaṁ
kara bhāṣagetappuvarāyaragaṁḍa pūrvadakṣiṇapaścimasamudrādhīśvara yavanarājya
sthāpanācārya gajapativibhāḍa śrīvīrapratāpa śrī kṛṣṇadēvamahārāyalu vijayana
garānanuṁḍi pūrvadigvijayayātraku viccēśi Udayagiridurgaṁ sādhiṁci tiru
malakātarāyamahāpātruṁni paṭṭukoni Adgaṁki vinukoṁḍa bellaṁkoṁḍa nāgārjununi koṁḍa ta
ṁgēḍa kētavaraṁ mōdalaina giridurggasthaladurggālu Ēkadhāṭini kaikoni koṁḍavīḍudurggaṁ mī
da viḍiśi laggalupaṭṭiṁcci durggaṁ puccukoni pratāparudragajapatirāya komāruṁḍu vīrabha
drarāyaḍu kumārapātruni koḍuku naraharidēvu rācūri mallūkhānuḍu Udaṁḍakhānu
ḍu jaṁnāla kasavāpātruṁḍu . pūsapāṭi rācirāju śrīnātharāju lakṣmīpatirāju paścima bālacaṁ
dramahāpātruḍu vīrumōdalaina pātrasāmaṁtulānu maṁnevāriniṁni jīvagrāhaṁgānu paṭṭukoni
Abhayadānaṁ vāriki Icci dharaṇikōṭa Amarēśvarānaku viccēśi kṛṣṇavēṇīnadītīramaṁdu Amarēśva
radēvunisaṁnidhini tulāpuruṣamahādānaṁ cēyanavadhariṁci tamadēvulu ciṁnādēviAmmagāri cē
tanu ratnadhēnumahādānamuṁnu tirumaladēviAṁmagāricētanu saptasāgaramahādāna muṁnu
cēyiṁpanavadhariṁci Aṁduṁḍi śrīparvatānaku viccēśi Ī dēvuni tēruvīdhi śilāmaṁṭapālu kaṭṭiṁciri
Ā śrīmukha saṁvatsara vaiśākha śu 11 guruvārānanu madhyāhnamavasaraṁ mahānaivēdyānaku pārumaṁcā
la Aneḍigrāmamuṁnu Ātukūru Aneḍigrāmamuṁnu samarpiṁciri kaṁcikāvaḍi suṁkaṁ gurraṁ yeddu gā
ḍida talamōpu modalainavāricēta suṁkaṁ kosakuṁḍānu mānistimi Iṁttavaṭṭu Ā caṁdrārkaṁ naḍavaṁga
lavāraṁ yavvaru tappakanaḍapiri Ā puṇyapuruṣula pādālu mā śirassunanu dhariṁcēmu . Ī
dharmaṁ yavvaru hānitalaci naḍaparairi Ā pāpakarmulu tama tallitaṁḍrulānu brāhma lānu
guruvulānu kāśilōnu gaṁgātīramaṁdu caṁpina pāpāna pōṁgalavāru . svadattā dvigu
ṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānupālanaṁ . paradattāpahārēṇa svada ttaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt . sāmānyō
yaṁ dharmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ . sarvā nētān bhāvinaḥ pārthivē
ṁdrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ . śrīśrīśrī . Below this the following is engraved in larger characters: śrīmallikā¨rjunadēvuniki staḷagaraṇaṁ
Digital edition of SII 16.52 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
